commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is needed; this is ready for normal maintainer PR validation and merge judgment.; Cleared: The diff only changes Ollama provider metadata and a colocated test assertion; it does not touch secrets, dependency resolution, CI, install scripts, or code-download paths. (sha=25ddb03fb9ee042fc534816183e5ab95612e9d29)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Ollama unknown-capabilities fallback to emit `compat.supportsTools: true` and adds a regression assertion for that fallback model definition.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence end-to-end reproduction was established in this read-only review. Source inspection does confirm that current main omits explicit `supportsTools` metadata on the unknown-capabilities fallback, and the PR body includes an after-patch fallback probe.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; this is ready for normal maintainer PR validation and merge judgment.

target.title: fix(ollama): default unknown capabilities to tools
target.body:
## Summary

Ollama models can lose tools when OpenClaw cannot read their capability list.
This change makes that fallback assume native Ollama chat supports tools.
It keeps the fix limited to the one issue reproduced from #81915.

## What Changed

The fallback model definition now marks unknown Ollama capabilities as tool-capable.
Models that explicitly report capabilities still use the reported tools flag.

- Set supportsTools to true when capabilities are unavailable.
- Added a regression assertion for that fallback path.

## Testing

I tested the exact fallback path and the focused Ollama provider model test file.
I also checked formatting and whitespace.

- node --import tsx --eval 'import { buildOllamaModelDefinition } from "./extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts"; const m = buildOllamaModelDefinition("qwen2.5:3b"); console.log(JSON.stringify(m.compat)); if (m.compat?.supportsTools !== true) process.exit(1);'
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.test.ts
- git diff --check

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw omitted supportsTools when Ollama capabilities were unknown.
Real environment tested: Local source checkout on Linux arm64, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.1.0, and local Ollama 0.24.0 for the PR #81915 investigation.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node --import tsx fallback probe and node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.test.ts.
Evidence after fix: The fallback probe printed {"supportsTools":true,"supportsUsageInStreaming":true}; provider-models.test.ts passed 13 tests.
Observed result after fix: Unknown Ollama capabilities now keep tools enabled by default.
What was not tested: I did not change or retest the unrelated idle-timeout, thinking, or keep_alive behavior from #81915 because only the tools fallback reproduced on current main.

## Risks

The main risk is an Ollama model that truly rejects tools when capabilities cannot be read.
That case can still opt out with compat.supportsTools: false on the model entry.
target.author: dutifulbob

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
